children.name: ABC Theory Psychoeducation Micro-Intervention
children.description: A structured, client-centered explanation of Ellis's ABC model to correct the misattribution of emotional consequences (C) directly to activating events (A), clarifying that beliefs (B) mediate and directly cause C — delivered as a time-bound, foundational micro-intervention to foster early cognitive awareness and collaborative orientation to the cognitive-behavioral model.
children.applicable_when: A structured, client-centered explanation of Ellis's ABC model to correct the misattribution of emotional consequences (C) directly to activating events (A), clarifying that beliefs (B) mediate and directly cause C — delivered as a time-bound, foundational micro-intervention to foster early cognitive awareness and collaborative orientation to the cognitive-behavioral model.
selector_terms: client attributes distress directly to external events
client shows rigid 'if-then' thinking about performance and self-worth
client presents with emotion misattributed solely to A
therapist aims to introduce cognitive model before intervention
client can articulate a concrete recent life event

children.name: ABC Theory Psychoeducation Micro-Intervention
children.description: A time-bound, client-specific micro-intervention delivering foundational psychoeducation on the cognitive-behavioral ABC model (Activating event, Belief, Consequence) to foster early cognitive awareness in clients presenting with event-attributed distress. Designed for use in solution-building phase to establish shared understanding of the cognitive mediation mechanism — without evaluating belief content — as a prerequisite for later cognitive restructuring.
children.applicable_when: A time-bound, client-specific micro-intervention delivering foundational psychoeducation on the cognitive-behavioral ABC model (Activating event, Belief, Consequence) to foster early cognitive awareness in clients presenting with event-attributed distress. Designed for use in solution-building phase to establish shared understanding of the cognitive mediation mechanism — without evaluating belief content — as a prerequisite for later cognitive restructuring.

ABC Theory Psychoeducation Micro-Intervention

A time-bound, client-specific micro-intervention delivering foundational psychoeducation on the cognitive-behavioral ABC model (Activating event, Belief, Consequence) to foster early cognitive awareness in clients presenting with event-attributed distress. Designed for use in solution-building phase to establish shared understanding of the cognitive mediation mechanism — without evaluating belief content — as a prerequisite for later cognitive restructuring.

children.name: ABC模型下产婆术辩论微序列
children.description: 在合理情绪疗法（ABC）框架内，通过结构化苏格拉底式提问链（如‘这个想法的证据是什么？’‘最坏结果真的会发生吗？’‘有没有其他可能解释？’），引导来访者主动识别、质疑并修正非理性信念，促发认知觉察与自我驳斥。专用于认知重构阶段的核心对话操作。
children.applicable_when: 在合理情绪疗法（ABC）框架内，通过结构化苏格拉底式提问链（如‘这个想法的证据是什么？’‘最坏结果真的会发生吗？’‘有没有其他可能解释？’），引导来访者主动识别、质疑并修正非理性信念，促发认知觉察与自我驳斥。专用于认知重构阶段的核心对话操作。

表达对某信念的轻微怀疑（如‘也许没那么糟？’）
在肌肉放松练习后报告躯体紧张度下降

在合理情绪疗法（ABC）框架内，通过结构化苏格拉底式提问链（如‘这个想法的证据是什么？’‘最坏结果真的会发生吗？’‘有没有其他可能解释？’），引导来访者主动识别、质疑并修正非理性信念，促发认知觉察与自我驳斥。专用于认知重构阶段的核心对话操作。
